name: PPF Calculator
metaTitle: PPF Calculator — Real Returns After Inflation | c7xai
metaDescription: Calculate PPF maturity with inflation-adjusted returns. See real value after 15 years. EEE tax benefit included. Client-side, no PII.

faq.question: How much will ₹1.5L/year PPF grow in 15 years?
faq.answer: A ₹1.5L/year PPF at 7.1% for 15 years grows to approximately ₹40.7 lakh nominally. But after 6% average inflation, the real value is about ₹16.9 lakh — you more than double your money in real terms thanks to the EEE tax benefit.

faq.question: Is PPF better than FD?
faq.answer: PPF beats FD for two reasons: (1) PPF is EEE — no tax on contribution, interest, or maturity. FD interest is taxed at your slab rate, which can reduce a 7% FD to ~5% post-tax. (2) PPF has a 15-year lock-in that forces discipline. But FD offers liquidity — you can break it with a small penalty.

faq.question: Can I extend PPF after 15 years?
faq.answer: Yes, you can extend PPF in 5-year blocks indefinitely. During extension, you can continue contributing (and earn interest on the full balance) or stop contributions (and just earn interest on the existing balance).

faq.question: What is the PPF interest rate for 2026?
faq.answer: PPF interest rate is set by the government quarterly and currently stands at 7.1%. It's linked to government bond yields and typically stays between 7-8%. The rate can change every quarter, but for planning purposes, use 7.1%.

howToSteps.name: Set yearly contribution
howToSteps.text: Enter how much you invest in PPF each year (max ₹1.5 lakh)

howToSteps.name: Set tenure
howToSteps.text: Choose 15 years (minimum) or longer with extensions

howToSteps.name: See real vs nominal maturity
howToSteps.text: Compare what your PPF grows to vs what it's actually worth after inflation
